meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Overcome 'No Portfolio' for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to build production-grade SaaS apps using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led, intermediate tutorial.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building tutorials and start shipping SaaS. By leveraging a high-performance starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can scaffold production-ready architecture in hours, transforming complex engineering requirements into a live, impressive product that proves your technical authority to future employers.
content.intro:
You stare at the screen, a cursor blinking in the void of a blank repository. You have the skills, but your GitHub profile is a graveyard of 'To-Do List' apps and outdated tutorials. The industry doesn't care about your coursework; it cares about your ability to ship. The tension between knowing how to code and actually proving you can build a sustainable SaaS product is the single biggest barrier to your career.

But here is the secret: you don't need to reinvent the wheel. By adopting a professional SaaS starter kit, you leapfrog the boilerplate phase and jump straight into architectural design and feature implementation. This isn't just about writing code; it's about mastering the workflow that elite engineers use to deploy scalable, high-conversion applications in record time.

steps.title: Architect the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Define your SaaS foundation using a robust starter kit template to ensure database, auth, and billing are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 15 project with Supabase Auth, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS, focusing on a clean, scalable directory structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with next-auth and postgres schema'

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Secure your application by defining granular user permissions, a critical skill for any enterprise SaaS project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware layer for role-based routing that restricts access based on user subscription tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add middleware for protected routes with subscription validation'

steps.title: Build the AI-Powered Feature
steps.description: Integrate an LLM layer that solves a real-world problem, demonstrating your ability to work with modern AI APIs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Construct an API route using the Vercel AI SDK to stream responses from Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet to the frontend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement streaming chat interface with AI SDK'

steps.title: Optimize Database Performance
steps.description: Move beyond basic CRUD by implementing efficient indexing and optimized query patterns for your SaaS data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational schema for user projects and generate optimized Drizzle ORM queries for high-frequency access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate drizzle schema and optimize user project queries'

steps.title: Finalize Production Deployment
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is tested, linted, and ready for global delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always build for 'Day 2' operations—prioritize error logging and observability over flashy UI components.
Use Feature Flags to decouple deployment from release, allowing you to test in production safely.
Focus your portfolio on the 'Why'—document the technical trade-offs you made in your README, not just the features.
Implement rate limiting on your API routes immediately; it demonstrates an understanding of production-grade security.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a living codebase, updating dependencies monthly to stay sharp on modern tooling.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, write a 'Post-Mortem' blog post detailing one specific engineering challenge you solved during this build, then link it prominently in your LinkedIn featured section.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a professional developer. You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap to turn 'no portfolio' into a competitive advantage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
